gedcom-x

GedcomX

1. Top Level Types

Person

A person in the system.

param id:

Unique identifier for this person.

type id:

str

param name:

Full name.

type name:

str

param birth:

Birth date (YYYY-MM-DD).

type birth:

date

param friends:

List of friends. Defaults to None.

type friends:

List[Person], optional

raises ValueError:

If id is not a valid UUID.

Calling Create a Person class with QuickPerson
1from gedcomx import QuickPerson
2
3# Create a Person class with QuickPerson
4mother = QuickPerson('My Mothers Name',dob='1/5/1939')

Relationship

Represents a relationship between two Person(s)

param type:

Type of relationship

type type:

RelationshipType

param person1:

type person1:

Person

param person2:

Second Person in Relationship

type person2:

Person

Raises:

SourceDescription

Description of a genealogical information source.

See: http://gedcomx.org/v1/SourceDescription

param id:

Unique identifier for this SourceDescription.

type id:

str | None

param resourceType:

Type/category of the resource being described (e.g., digital artifact, physical artifact).

type resourceType:

ResourceType | None

param citations:

Citations that reference or justify this source description.

type citations:

list[SourceCitation] | None

param mediaType:

IANA media (MIME) type of the resource (e.g., "application/pdf").

type mediaType:

str | None

param about:

Canonical URI that the description is about.

type about:

URI | None

param mediator:

The mediator resource (if any) involved in providing access to the source.

type mediator:

Resource | None

param publisher:

Publisher of the resource.

type publisher:

Resource | Agent | None

param authors:

Authors/creators of the resource.

type authors:

list[Resource] | None

param sources:

Other sources this description derives from or references.

type sources:

list[SourceReference] | None

param analysis:

Analysis document associated with the resource (often a Document; kept generic to avoid circular imports).

type analysis:

Resource | None

param componentOf:

Reference to a parent/containing source (this is a component/child of that source).

type componentOf:

SourceReference | None

param titles:

One or more titles for the resource.

type titles:

list[TextValue] | None

param notes:

Human-authored notes about the resource.

type notes:

list[Note] | None

param attribution:

Attribution metadata for who supplied or curated this description.

type attribution:

Attribution | None

param rights:

Rights statements or licenses.

type rights:

list[Resource] | None

param coverage:

Spatial/temporal coverage of the source’s content.

type coverage:

list[Coverage] | None

param descriptions:

Short textual summaries or descriptions.

type descriptions:

list[TextValue] | None

param identifiers:

Alternative identifiers for the resource (DOI, ARK, call numbers, etc.).

type identifiers:

IdentifierList | None

param created:

Creation date of the resource.

type created:

Date | None

param modified:

Last modified date of the resource.

type modified:

Date | None

param published:

Publication/release date of the resource.

type published:

Date | None

param repository:

Repository or agency that holds the resource.

type repository:

Agent | None

param max_note_count:

Maximum number of notes to retain/emit. Defaults to 20.

type max_note_count:

int

raises ValueError:

If id is not a valid UUID.

identifier

Gedcom-X specification identifier for this type.

Type:

str

Agent

class Agent(id=None, identifiers=None, names=[], homepage=None, openid=None, accounts=[], emails=[], phones=[], addresses=[], person=None, attribution=None)

Bases: object

A GedcomX Agent Data Type.

Represents an agent entity such as a person, organization, or software responsible for creating or modifying genealogical data, as defined in the GedcomX conceptual model.

Static Methods:

Parameters:

  • id (str, optional) – A unique identifier for this agent. If not provided, one may be generated automatically using default_id_generator().

  • identifiers (IdentifierList, optional) – A list of alternate identifiers for this agent.

  • names (List[TextValue], optional) – Names associated with the agent. Defaults to an empty list.

  • homepage (URI, optional) – A link to the agent’s homepage or primary website.

  • openid (URI, optional) – The OpenID identifier for the agent.

  • accounts (List[OnlineAccount], optional) – Online accounts associated with the agent. Defaults to an empty list.

  • emails (List[URI], optional) – Email addresses associated with the agent. Defaults to an empty list.

  • phones (List[Resource], optional) – Phone numbers associated with the agent. Defaults to an empty list.

  • addresses (List[Address], optional) – Postal addresses associated with the agent. Defaults to an empty list.

  • person (Person, optional) – A reference to the person represented by the agent. Accepts a Person object or a Resource reference. Declared as object to avoid circular imports.

  • attribution (Attribution, optional) – Attribution information related to the agent.

  • uri (Resource, optional) – A URI reference for this agent.

add_address(address_to_add)
Parameters:

address_to_add (Address)

add_identifier(identifier_to_add)
Parameters:

identifier_to_add (Identifier)

add_name(name_to_add)
Parameters:

name_to_add (TextValue)

add_note(note_to_add)

Event

Represents a conclusion in the GEDCOM X conceptual model. A conclusion is a genealogical assertion about a person, relationship, or event, derived from one or more sources, with optional supporting metadata such as confidence, attribution, and notes.

param id:

A unique identifier for the conclusion. If not provided, a UUID-based identifier will be automatically generated.

type id:

str, optional

param lang:

The language code of the conclusion.

type lang:

str, optional

param sources:

A list of source references that support the conclusion.

type sources:

list[SourceReference], optional

param analysis:

A reference to an analysis document or resource that supports the conclusion.

type analysis:

Document | Resource, optional

param notes:

A list of notes providing additional context. Defaults to an empty list.

type notes:

list[Note], optional

param confidence:

The contributor’s confidence in the conclusion (High, Medium, or Low).

type confidence:

ConfidenceLevel, optional

param attribution:

Information about who contributed the conclusion and when.

type attribution:

Attribution, optional

param uri:

A URI reference for the conclusion. Defaults to a URI with the fragment set to the id.

type uri:

Resource, optional

param links:

A list of links associated with the conclusion. Defaults to an empty _LinkList.

type links:

_LinkList, optional

Document

class Document(id=None, lang=None, sources=None, analysis=None, notes=None, confidence=None, attribution=None, type=None, extracted=None, textType=None, text=None)

Bases: Conclusion

Parameters:

  • id (str | None)

  • lang (str | None)

  • sources (List[SourceReference] | None)

  • analysis (Resource | None)

  • notes (List[Note] | None)

  • confidence (ConfidenceLevel | None)

  • attribution (Attribution | None)

  • type (DocumentType | None)

  • extracted (bool | None)

  • textType (TextType | None)

  • text (str | None)

identifier = 'http://gedcomx.org/v1/Document'
version = 'http://gedcomx.org/conceptual-model/v1'
class DocumentType(*values)

Bases: Enum

Abstract = 'http://gedcomx.org/Abstract'
Analysis = 'http://gedcomx.org/Analysis'
Transcription = 'http://gedcomx.org/Transcription'
Translation = 'http://gedcomx.org/Translation'
property description
class TextType(*values)

Bases: Enum

plain = 'plain'
xhtml = 'xhtml'

PlaceDescription

PlaceDescription describes the details of a place in terms of its name and possibly its type, time period, and/or a geospatial description functioning as a description of a place as a snapshot in time.

Encapsulates textual names, geospatial coordinates, jurisdictional context, temporal coverage, and related resources (media, sources, evidence, etc.).

names

Human-readable names or labels for the place (e.g., “Boston, Suffolk, Massachusetts, United States”).

Type:

Optional[List[TextValue]]

type

A place type identifier (e.g., a URI). TODO: replace with an enumeration when finalized.

Type:

Optional[str]

place

Canonical identifier (URI) for the place.

Type:

Optional[URI]

jurisdiction

The governing or containing jurisdiction of this place (e.g., county for a town).

Type:

Optional[Resource|PlaceDescription]

latitude

Latitude in decimal degrees (WGS84).

Type:

Optional[float]

longitude

Longitude in decimal degrees (WGS84).

Type:

Optional[float]

temporalDescription

Temporal coverage/validity window for this description (e.g., when a jurisdictional boundary applied).

Type:

Optional[Date]

spatialDescription

A resource describing spatial geometry or a link to an external gazetteer/shape definition.

Type:

Optional[Resource]

This example shows basic usage:

Calling my_function
1from gedcomx import GedcomX, Attribution, Agent
2
3# get the answer
4answer = my_function(42, key="value")
5print(f"Answer is {answer}")
